steps.title: Identify what the blank needs
steps.content: Words in Context: the blank describes what reviewers, booksellers, and librarians did to the book. Two context clues constrain the answer: (1) the reception has been "very good," and (2) the book won a major literary award. The blank must be a word meaning something positive these stakeholders do when they endorse a book.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘attacked’: Means to criticize harshly — the opposite of a very good reception. Substituting: "many reviewers have attacked the book... and it won an award" is contradictory. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘overlooked’: Means to ignore or fail to notice — incompatible with a good reception and an award win. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘recommended’: Means to endorse or suggest positively — exactly what reviewers, booksellers, and librarians do when reception is very good. Substituting: "many reviewers have recommended the book... and it won an award" fits perfectly. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘misunderstood’: Means to interpret incorrectly — a negative or neutral outcome, not consistent with a very good reception. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
